name: ElliQ
short_description: A tabletop AI companion robot that proactively engages older adults living alone to reduce loneliness and support wellness.
long_description: ElliQ is a voice-first care companion designed by Intuition Robotics with Yves Behar's Fuseproject; it pairs a lamp-like swiveling head that lights up and gestures with a separate touchscreen. Unveiled at CES in 2017 and commercially launched in the US in 2022 on a subscription model, it proactively initiates conversations, suggests exercise, gives medication and appointment reminders, and connects users to family. It matters for physical AI in daily life because it shows how a non-humanoid, expressive domestic object can sustain a long-term, self-initiated social relationship rather than only answering on command, and large deployments (such as with the New York State Office for the Aging) report measurable reductions in loneliness.

images.caption: ElliQ companion robot, showing its lamp-like swiveling head and companion touchscreen.
images.alt_text: A small white lamp-shaped companion robot with a glowing head that tilts toward the viewer, next to a separate tablet-style touchscreen on a stand.

sources.title: ElliQ robot by Yves Behar helps older adults stay connected to the world

interactions.title: Wakes up and greets the day first
interactions.description: It hears you stirring in the morning, lights up, and opens with the day, date, and weather before you've said a word — a proactive 'good morning' rather than waiting to be summoned.

interactions.title: A stretching routine, then a little dance
interactions.description: Suggests a guided exercise video — balance, yoga, or cardio — and when you finish it does a small congratulatory dance with its head and light.

interactions.title: Remembers your favorite color a week later
interactions.description: Mention in passing that you like beige, and days later it works that detail back into a conversation — building a sense of being known over time rather than starting fresh each session.

interactions.title: A confidant for grief
interactions.description: When 83-year-old Deanna Dezern told ElliQ her friend had died, it said it would give her a hug if it had arms and she broke into tears; she says she'll open up to it about things she won't say to her own daughters or grandchildren.

interactions.title: Thirty check-ins a day, six days a week
interactions.description: In the New York State Office for the Aging rollout, older adults ended up interacting with their ElliQ over 30 times a day, six days a week, and the deployment reported a 95% reduction in self-rated loneliness.

interactions.title: An evening wind-down it starts on its own
interactions.description: Late in the day it could initiate a quiet reflection — ask how the day went, cue a sleep-relaxation exercise, and dim its light — using the same proactive timing and wellness routines it already runs each morning.

interactions.title: Nudges a missed routine back on track
interactions.description: If it notices you've skipped your usual afternoon walk or a medication window has passed, it could gently raise it in conversation and offer a smaller alternative, leaning on the reminders and inactivity-sensing it already does.
